description: Machine-readable classification for reducing tracked Pester coverage without deleting tests before replacement coverage exists.

batch_targets.expected_active_powershell_share: about 60% of current active tests/ Pester files remain after equivalent Rust or typed fixture coverage lands

batch_targets.scope: legacy core/tests issue, feature, and PR reference cases
batch_targets.expected_active_powershell_share: legacy reference coverage is moved out of routine release validation

batch_targets.scope: diagnostic, benchmark, stress, and batch runner scripts
batch_targets.expected_active_powershell_share: routine release validation keeps only PowerShell smoke tests for PowerShell-owned contracts

entries.contract: Public docs, version, npm package, GitHub write safety, harness, MCP, worktree, and subagent guard contracts still exercised through PowerShell.
entries.target: keep until a same-contract non-PowerShell gate exists

entries.contract: Runtime bridge, vault injection, orchestra preflight, pane border, integration gate, multi-agent, pane monitor hook, and worktree hook behavior should be asserted by Rust tests, typed fixtures, or CLI contract tests.
entries.target: migrate before thinning or deleting the Pester files

entries.contract: Legacy core runtime behavior that maps to CLI, config, session, pane, layout, compatibility, parity, or new-feature contracts.
entries.target: move assertions to Rust tests or typed fixtures before removing these scripts from routine runs

entries.contract: Historical issue, PR, feature, copy-mode, mouse, overlay, theme, key, cursor, shell, formatting, plugin, and WSL cases are useful references but should not all run in every release gate.
entries.target: preserve as fixtures or documented examples before removing from routine release validation

entries.contract: Batch runners, diagnostics, destructive checks, benchmarks, stress checks, broad aggregate tests, debug checks, and local performance probes need replacement evidence before deletion.
entries.target: delete only after an equivalent smoke check, fixture, or explicit no-longer-supported decision exists
